title: Reputation grid and road grid overlay systems
description:
Implement src/world/reputation.rs: A grid overlay (cell_size ~8px) with 4 independent signal channels (PROFIT, DEMAND, DANGER, OPPORTUNITY). Each channel is a 2D array of f32 (world_size / cell_size resolution, ~200x125). Per tick: (1) deposit — merchants write signals additively, clamped at 1.0; (2) diffusion — separable Gaussian blur per channel with configurable sigma; (3) decay — multiply by channel-specific decay factor. Provide bilinear interpolation sampling at arbitrary world positions. Scanner sampling: given position and heading, sample average signal in left/right cones (±35° from heading, range 60px). Compute gradient vectors for PROFIT and DANGER channels at a position. Target: reputation processing < 2ms per tick — use flat arrays, avoid heap allocation in hot loop.

Implement src/world/road.rs: Same grid resolution. Each cell has a road_value (f32, 0.0-1.0). Traversal: when a merchant crosses a cell, increment road_value by +0.002 (clamped at 1.0). Decay: multiply all cells by 0.9998 per tick. Speed bonus: 1.0 + 0.6 × road_value. Provide method to get road speed multiplier at world position.
result_summary:
Both modules are implemented and all tests pass. Here's a summary:

### `src/world/reputation.rs` — ReputationGrid
- **4 independent channels** (Profit, Demand, Danger, Opportunity) as flat `Vec<f32>` arrays (200x125 at default config)
- **Deposit**: additive write at world position, clamped to 1.0
- **Diffusion**: separable Gaussian blur per channel with pre-computed 1D kernels; reuses a single scratch buffer (zero heap allocation in tick)
- **Decay**: per-channel multiplicative decay
- **Bilinear interpolation** sampling at arbitrary world positions (cell-centered)
- **Scanner sampling**: 3x3 polar grid (9 samples per cone) in left/right cones at ±half_angle from heading, up to `range` distance
- **Gradient**: central-difference approximation for any channel at a world position

### `src/world/road.rs` — RoadGrid
- Flat `Vec<f32>` array, same resolution as reputation grid
- **Traverse**: increments cell by config increment (0.002), clamped to 1.0
- **Decay**: multiplies all cells by decay factor (0.9998) per tick
- **Speed multiplier**: `1.0 + max_speed_bonus * road_value` at query position
- Out-of-bounds positions return 0.0 / multiplier 1.0
